subject: Anthropology
title: Hate Speech and Language
description: One-minute skill: Hate Speech and Language.

hook.text: Words can dehumanize. Before every genocide, language was used to turn people into vermin, insects, or diseases.

buildup.text: Hate speech uses language to degrade, threaten, or dehumanize groups based on identity markers like race or religion.

discovery.text: Linguistic anthropologists study how dehumanizing metaphors prepare populations to accept violence against others.

twist.text: The line between free and harmful speech varies across cultures. What's legal in one country is banned in another.

climax.text: Language can build bridges or burn them. Understanding hate speech reveals the destructive power words can carry.

punchline.text: Words can wound before weapons arrive.

quiz.question: How do dehumanizing metaphors function?
options: They are harmless figures of speech
They prepare populations to accept violence against groups
